entries.title: Config cache reset pattern for test isolation
entries.description:
# Config cache reset pattern for test isolation

## Context

Captured during development session on 2026-02-21

## Pattern

The intent classifier caches parsed routing-config.yaml in a module-level variable for performance. Tests that modify routing behavior must call resetConfigCache() before each test to prevent cross-test contamination. This pattern applies to any module that caches config at import time. In vitest, use beforeEach(() => resetConfigCache()) in test setup. The flow-parser similarly caches loaded flows and must be tested with fresh imports or cache-busting.

## Example

```typescript
import { resetConfigCache } from '../runtime/intent-classifier.js'; beforeEach(() => resetConfigCache());
```

## Why

Module-level caching is good for performance but creates hidden state in tests. Explicit cache reset ensures test independence.

entries.title: Domain vocabulary extension pattern for new problem spaces
entries.description:
# Domain vocabulary extension pattern for new problem spaces

## Context

Captured during development session on 2026-02-21

## Pattern

New domains are added by creating a YAML file in chains/domains/ following the ui-components.yaml template. Each domain defines: context-keywords (terms that trigger context detection), entity-synonyms (aliases like modal→dialog), classification levels, and chain-preferences (which chains are relevant per context). After adding a domain file, update routing-config.yaml context-keywords section to reference the new terms. No code changes needed — the runtime reads domains dynamically.

## Example

```typescript
New domain: domains/backend-api.yaml with keywords [endpoint, route, api, service, backend] and entity-synonyms [endpoint→route, handler→controller]
```

## Why

Domain vocabularies make the system adaptable to new problem spaces without modifying core logic. Each domain is a pluggable knowledge module.

entries.title: Escape hatch strategy: structured recovery from stuck states
entries.description:
# Escape hatch strategy: structured recovery from stuck states

## Context

Captured during development session on 2026-02-21

## Pattern

Salvador defines explicit escape hatches in CLAUDE.md for common stuck states. 'Same error 3x' → change-approach (don't retry the same thing). 'Context growing' → make-smaller (decompose the task). 'Can't define done' → clarify-objective (stop and ask). 'Fighting the system' → step-back (reassess the approach). On final failure, escalate with structured task-format handoff. The recovery flow (flows/recovery.yaml) implements these as formal steps: diagnose → isolate → plan-fix → implement-fix → verify-fix → regression-check.

## Example

```typescript
IF-STUCK: match 'same error 3x' → action: change-approach. match 'fighting system' → action: step-back.
```

## Why

Automated workflows without escape hatches spiral into infinite retries. Explicit recovery strategies bound failure modes.

entries.title: Below-threshold loop-custom
entries.description:
# Below-threshold loop-custom

## Context

Automatically promoted from proposed knowledge after being observed in 150 sessions.
Flow: loop-custom
## Anti-Pattern

Session 2026-02-27-029a6dfc scored 30 (grade: F), which is below the acceptable threshold. Review the approach and gate failures for improvement opportunities.

## Evidence

- Observed in 150 sessions across flow `loop-custom`
- Promoted: 2026-03-02
- Source rule: `low-score`
